events.event_description: U.S. diplomatic mission to Turkey established when David Porter presented credentials as Chargé d'Affaires, representing the first U.S. diplomatic presence in Turkey.

evidence.quote: David Porter, Chargé d'Affaires. Appointed: 1831-04-15. Credentials presented: 1831-09-13.

events.event_description: U.S. mission elevated from Legation to Embassy when John G A Leishman was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented new credentials.

evidence.quote:
John G A Leishman, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1900-12-20. Credentials presented: 1901-03-29. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1906-10-05.

John G A Leishman, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1906-06-18. Credentials presented: 1906-10-05.

events.event_description: Turkey severed diplomatic relations with the United States, ending the U.S. Embassy presence.

evidence.quote: Turkey severed diplomatic relations with the United States on 1917-04-20. Note: Elkus left post on May 29, 1917.

events.event_description: U.S. Embassy re-established in Turkey when Joseph Clark Grew presented credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, restoring diplomatic relations after the 1917 break.

evidence.quote: Joseph Clark Grew, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1927-05-19. Credentials presented: 1927-10-12.
